title: euphorbia helioscopia f. litoralis
intro: Euphorbia helioscopia f. litoralis, also known as seaside spurge, is a subspecies of the spurge family native to coastal areas in Europe and North Africa. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral compounds that can help to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing. The health benefits of Euphorbia helioscopia f. litoralis include the treatment of skin conditions such as eczema and acne, as well as its potential to reduce symptoms of arthritis and other inflammatory diseases. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or ointments, and are often used topically to apply the medicinal properties directly to the affected area.
